_comment: Sparse-input-pass fixture. Hypothetical US student with firstToApplyAbroad='yes'. Card 01 includes one of the 'first_gen_college' framing markers. Locks Test 7 for US students — they MUST get standard first-gen-college framing, NOT the 'first to leave home' angle (which is reserved for CIS / MENA per cultural-context.ts).

intake.topActivity: student council president, immigration clinic volunteer
intake.background: first-generation, raised in El Paso, parents immigrated from Juarez
intake.extracurriculars: student council president 2 years, immigration legal clinic 200 hours, debate state semifinalist

dbContext: London School of Economics (UK) — public policy + government powerhouse, strong financial aid for US applicants. SOAS University of London (UK) — international relations + development focus. Hertie School (Germany) — English-medium public-policy master's-style undergrad option. University of Amsterdam (Netherlands) — strong politics + international relations programs in English. Leiden University (Netherlands) — international studies in The Hague campus.

archetype.reason: intake names a clear major + sustained domain commitment (student council + clinic) — Tight Lane archetype fits

plan.identityClaim: a public-policy kid who already knows the work
plan.pileContrast: Most kids in your year name 'PoliSci' as a major because it sounds serious, not because they've sat in a clinic for 200 hours.
plan.essaySeedType: a single client moment from the immigration clinic that changed how she frames the work

primaryGap.reason: Maya picked 3 countries but they're all Europe — tunnel-vision-3 lane risk; widen consideration to programs that fit her file.

plan.mondayMoveArtifact: a Google Doc titled 'one clinic story' listing three specific client encounters that mattered

whereYouStand.headline: You're a public-policy kid who already knows the work.
whereYouStand.lead: Maya, your file reads like someone who's been doing this for years.
whereYouStand.body: Most kids in your year name PoliSci as a major because it sounds serious — they haven't sat in a clinic for 200 hours. You have. Student council president for two years, immigration legal clinic intake worker, debate state semifinalist — that's a specific track most admissions readers don't see at your school level. And you'd be the first-generation college applicant going abroad — first in your family to attend university outside the US. That's not a footnote; that's a framing the application benefits from.
whereYouStand.pullquote: The clinic hours aren't a side project — they're the spine of the file.

whereYouCanLand.headline: Three kinds of places where the clinic hours are the headline.
whereYouCanLand.lead: Pulled from your countries, with your file in mind.

schools.name: London School of Economics
schools.lore: Where 'I worked in a legal clinic for 200 hours' lands as the headline, not the side note. They're built to read first-gen public-policy applicants well.

schools.name: Hertie School
schools.lore: English-medium, fee-free public-policy track. The Berlin location adds context to a first-gen application that names policy as the through-line.

schools.name: University of Amsterdam
schools.lore: Strong politics + international-relations track in English, with a clear path for students whose application is already domain-specific.

whatToWrite.kicker: 03 · The essay only you can write
whatToWrite.headline: The essay you should write doesn't exist yet — but the seed for it does.
whatToWrite.lead: One specific kind of moment from the clinic. Then it starts itself.

essaySeed.title: The one client moment
essaySeed.body: Sometime in the last two years, a client in the immigration clinic said something that changed how Maya thought about the work. Maybe a question that didn't have a clean answer. Probably a moment where the legal form Maya was helping with felt insufficient. Maybe a phrase the client used that didn't translate the way the intake sheet expected. Find that exact moment — the one where Maya's idea of public-policy got more specific.

whatsBlockingYou.headline: All three target countries are in Europe.
whatsBlockingYou.lead: Tunnel-vision risk — widen the consideration set, not the application list.

entries.title: Three-country tunnel vision
entries.whyItMatters: Maya, the UK + Germany + Netherlands set reads like one geography. For a first-gen-college applicant with strong clinic hours, the file plays equally well in Canada or Ireland — programs that explicitly recruit US first-gen applicants for policy depth.
entries.actionThisMonth: Add two countries to the consideration set this week — even just reading their public-policy programs adds context. The application list stays narrow; the consideration set widens.
entries.next60Days: Decide whether one of the wider countries actually fits, or whether the Europe-3 framing was the right call after all.

whatToDoThisMonth.headline: Open a Google Doc titled 'one clinic story' this week.
whatToDoThisMonth.lead: One move that opens the rest.

mondayMove.headline: Open a Google Doc titled 'one clinic story' this week.
mondayMove.body: Inside, list three specific client encounters from the immigration clinic that mattered. Don't polish. Don't make them sound profound. Just three lines, one per encounter. Stop when you have three.
mondayMove.closer: Once those three lines exist, the essay has somewhere to start.
